methods.description: Northern blot analysis was used to assess the transcript levels of ZmPti1b in infected maize kernels to determine the impact of Fusarium infection on gene expression.

steps.description: ZmPti1b transcript level in infected kernels isolated from a cob possessing a disease severity 6 indicating 51%-75% of infection.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate RNA from infected maize kernels

steps.description: Northern blot analysis revealed a four-fold enhanced ZmPti1b transcript level.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Northern blot to analyze ZmPti1b transcript levels

methods.method_name: Plant Protein Extraction and Immunoblot Analysis
methods.description: This method describes the extraction of proteins from plant tissues and subsequent immunoblot analysis to study protein expression.

steps.description: Frozen tissues were ground in a MM301 ball mill and proteins were solubilized for 1 h at 4°C in extraction buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: grind plant tissues and solubilize proteins in extraction buffer

steps.description: Protein extract was cleared by centrifugation and protein concentration was determined using Bradford reagent.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge protein extract and determine concentration

steps.description: Proteins were fractionated by SDS-PAGE and transferred onto ECL-Nitrocellulose membranes by electroblotting.
Immunodetection was performed using standard ECL system procedures.
